item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not 100% sure exactly what just happened, though I have some semi-solid theories, but goddamn if it wasn't well written and captivating the whole way through.

+1 for making me probably run out of time to finish my French studies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think the the skip offers enough details for me to draw a solid conclusion. It feels too open-ended especially now that you imply that Dylan could be lying about a few things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a wonderfully captivating article from start to finish. I loved the initial set-up with Researcher Dylan typing up the article as the "last sane man" in the site (while definitely a set-up that's been done before in other SCPs, the mystery surrounding the cabinet and the rest of the researchers did a great job of capturing my interest regardless) and the exploration logs were enjoyable and well-written all the way through. Researcher Dylan in particular was a pretty likable narrator whose dialogue felt largely realistic; I definitely got a laugh out of him forgetting to turn the recorder on in the third exploration log, and small touches like that went a long way in fleshing him out as a character I could care about. My primary concern was whether the actual pay-off of the article would measure up to the quality of the exploration logs, but thankfully, the ambiguity of the ending and the unsettling mystery it presents was more than enough to satisfy me. My only real criticism would be that the audio logs, while definitely impressive, didn't add much to the article for me; in particular, I felt like the voice acting for Researcher Dylan wasn't super convincing, and I ended up reading all the exploration logs without the audio logs playing. Besides that, though, this was a great SCP through and through.

item.title: Re: Holy Shit
item.content:encoded:
Honesty, "best skip I've ever read" is the best compliment I can get as an SCP writer. Thanks a million!

As for the multiverse, I'd love to see some tales about it. In my view, Dylan only scratched the surface of what happened.

item.title: Holy Shit
item.content:encoded: This…this is the best skip I've ever read. Hats off to you! I'd love to make a similar skip, or at least expand upon the multiverse in which it takes place!

item.title: Some overdue appreciation
item.content:encoded:
Yo not_a_seagull, it's t7 from the IRC. Finally got around to giving it my +1. Well-deserved, too! The exploration logs communicate well in text, to the point that I'm legitimately afraid to listen to the audio logs for fear of spoiling the image they built up. The basic concept is really amazing and the presentation is better. Makes me want to work on my exploration draft!

Cheers, and thanks for the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While the overall story this SCP tells isn't something incredibly original and I can certainly remember more than one article with the similar concept, (093 comes to mind, as well as some other older SCPs) I still think that the execution is done very well, the logs are interesting and voice acting adds to the atmosphere. So I see more than enough reasons to upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unfortunately, I wasn't really able to get into it. The dialogue from the researcher and the notation regarding audio felt far too leading at times, taking away from atmosphere by way of tell over show and robbing me of the ability to really envision the setting without a ton of hand-holding.

I still have the audio projects, and I can definitely re-record some of the lines here. I had trouble making the dialogue not-explainy, as I wanted to make sure the world was clear. Where, specifically, were the problem areas? I can hopefully fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I interrupted it as the site's staff being infected and the AICs locking them out because of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was really, really into this from the start. It's a great premise, and the introduction of the doors gave me some strong 093 vibes - I was very excited to see how each exploration segued together and to explore the story world.

Unfortunately, I wasn't really able to get into it. The dialogue from the researcher and the notation regarding audio felt far too leading at times, taking away from atmosphere by way of tell over show and robbing me of the ability to really envision the setting without a ton of hand-holding.

Also, I'll admit I'm missing the significance of the alt-u's AICs acting up, how does that relate to anything? And further questioning if TSAT is supposed to have been similarly affected - if not, seems like a missed opportunity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article kicks ass, loved the format screw.
0+1

item.title: Re: Damn that was good!
item.content:encoded:
Oh believe me I know.

I hope you do keep going and post some more articles.
(PM links to anything else you posted)

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: If you or anyone need some OSCP voice done, don't hesitate to put some PMs through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoilers
– hide block

One of the things I tried to do with this skip was to create an ending where it’s up to interpretation. I don’t want to share the ending I had in mind while I was writing this, out of the fear that it will be accepted as the “official” ending, thus nullifying any reader-generated interpretations.

However, here’s some food for thought: Is Researcher Dylan a reliable narrator?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The premise is great, but I'm left a little confused on what actually happened. The ending feels a little rushed, it whipped past me and I don't think I picked up everything from it.

item.title: Re: Damn that was good!
item.content:encoded: Thanks for reading! These kind of comments really keep me going, y’know?

item.title: Damn that was good!
item.content:encoded:
Wow. That was amazing.

At first I was a down vote from seeing the use of first person but switched to upvote once I was able to understand what was going on.

Great job.
That was a lot of fun to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can see where this can come from, but I feel that the base format screw, the difference in base concepts, and more streamline style separate this a lot from 093. I can still understand why people would think this however, but I honestly think the presentation is the main thing separating the two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair. Honestly, SCP-093 is so good and completely revolutionized the exploration log scene in such a way that it's difficult to outdo it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a no vote on this one. It feels too much like a pale imitation of 093 for my liking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, hearing the fully compiled audio logs is surreal. Loved listening to this, and it's a very easy +1 from me. Appreciate the format screw as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay I'm so glad this is posted! Great work on everything, and I agree the audio logs help a lot. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely ecstatic to see this posted. The audio logs add a whole new level to this, and all of the updates to this from the draft I read have really solidified my upvote more than before. There have been so many good articles posted in the span of a week, it's insane.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Alright, I think that all the files, etc are in the right place now. This is probably the most effort I've ever put into a skip, and I hope that it's enjoyed.

Firstly, massive shoutout to TSATPWTCOTTTADC, pxdnbluesoul, and Proasek for doing voices for this skip.

Secondly, thank you to Thanks to DarkStuff, Meserach, Azmoeth1, Dysadron, Laneous, aismallard, AthosNetwork and mayoculpa for critique!

All sounds in this are either mine or one of my voice artists, except for the following:

+ show block
– hide block
  • Siren: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Siren_loop_(Carrai_Pass).wav
  • Motor: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Compressor_motor.ogg
  • OSCP Startup Sound: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sounding_waves.ogg
  • Glass Shatter: freesound .org/people/221Beimesche/sounds/336425/
  • Walking Sound: freesound .org/people/JohnsonBrandEditing/sounds/244310/

Put spaces in links that wikidot marks as "blacklisted" for some reason?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. +1
